undefined.name: Undefined
undefined.message: Undefined in diagnostic info usually indicates no issue here.
undefined.fixAdvice: You generally don't need to consider this diagnostic unless you encounter other abnormal behavior.

unknownError.name: Unknown Error
unknownError.message: An unknown error occurred, possibly due to an internal error or unhandled exception.
unknownError.fixAdvice: Please try restarting ASER Studio. If the problem persists, contact technical support and provide relevant logs.

ruleNotSelected.name: No Grammar Rule Selected
ruleNotSelected.message: Document %1 does not specify any grammar rule via #useRule directive.
ruleNotSelected.fixAdvice: Try adding #useRule:%1 on the second line of the document to specify the grammar rule defined in %1.

noSuchRule.name: Grammar Rule Does Not Exist
noSuchRule.message: The grammar rule %2 specified by #useRule directive in document %1 is invalid.
noSuchRule.fixAdvice: Check the parameter of #useRule directive in document %1 to ensure it correctly specifies an existing grammar rule. Currently valid rules are: %2

noSuchController.name: Controller Does Not Exist
noSuchController.fixAdvice: Check if your code correctly references the controller prefix specified by rule %1, ensure it is defined and spelled correctly.

missingRequiredParameter.name: Missing Required Parameter
missingRequiredParameter.message: Controller "%1 controller" is missing required parameter %2.
missingRequiredParameter.fixAdvice: Remove the symbol indicating optional parameter and refill the value starting from the line-start identifier.

parameterTypeMismatch.name: Parameter Type Mismatch
parameterTypeMismatch.message: Parameter %1 type mismatch, you may have provided type %2, but expected type is %3

parameterOutOfRange.name: Parameter Out of Range
parameterOutOfRange.message: Parameter %1 value is outside the allowed range.
parameterOutOfRange.fixAdvice: Check controller parameters to ensure the value is within the allowed range.

parameterFormatError.name: Parameter Format Error
parameterFormatError.fixAdvice: Check controller parameters to ensure the format meets expectations.

unexceptedParameter.name: Unexpected Parameter
unexceptedParameter.fixAdvice: Check the definition of controller %1 to ensure it only accepts expected parameters and no extra parameters are passed.

unclosedSymbol.name: Unclosed Symbol
unclosedSymbol.message: Symbol %2 in document %1 is not properly closed.
unclosedSymbol.fixAdvice: Check symbol %2 in document %1 and ensure they are properly closed. (Add %3 symbol at appropriate positions to close them.)

variableNotDefined.name: Variable Not Defined
variableNotDefined.fixAdvice: Check the spelling of %1, or ensure you have defined this variable earlier via statement %1.

useTabInsteadSpace.name: Use Tab Instead of Spaces
useTabInsteadSpace.message: Spaces used where tabs should be used.

useSpaceInsteadTab.name: Use Spaces Instead of Tabs
useSpaceInsteadTab.message: Tabs used where spaces should be used.
useSpaceInsteadTab.fixAdvice: Replace tabs with appropriate number of spaces.

useEnglishPunctuation.name: Use English Punctuation
useEnglishPunctuation.message: Chinese punctuation used where English punctuation is required.
useEnglishPunctuation.fixAdvice: Replace Chinese punctuation with corresponding English punctuation.

useChinesePunctuation.name: Use Chinese Punctuation
useChinesePunctuation.message: English punctuation used where Chinese punctuation is required.
useChinesePunctuation.fixAdvice: Replace English punctuation with corresponding Chinese punctuation.

noSuchBlock.name: Block Does Not Exist
noSuchBlock.fixAdvice: Check the spelling of %1, or ensure you have defined this block earlier via statement %1.

blockNameConflict.name: Block Name Conflict
blockNameConflict.message: Block %1 is already defined at line %2.
blockNameConflict.fixAdvice: Choose a different name for this block or the block defined at line %1.

instrumentRedundant.name: Redundant Directive
instrumentRedundant.message: Directive %1 is redundant here; it duplicates the directive at line %1

parameterRepeated.name: Parameter Repeated
parameterRepeated.fixAdvice: Check the controller's parameter list to ensure each parameter appears only once.

unknownWarning.name: Unknown Warning
unknownWarning.message: An unknown warning occurred, possibly due to an internal error or unhandled exception.
unknownWarning.fixAdvice: Please try restarting ASER Studio. If the problem persists, contact technical support and provide relevant logs.

nosuchResource.name: Resource Does Not Exist
nosuchResource.fixAdvice: Check the spelling of %1, or add a resource named %1 to your project.

useTabInsteadSpace_dialog.name: Use Tab Instead of Spaces in Dialog Controller
useTabInsteadSpace_dialog.message: In the dialog controller, use tabs to separate the speaker and dialogue content instead of spaces. If you are sure the spaces are part of the person's name, ignore this warning.

Preprocessor.__description: Preprocessor directives, used to process the document during the pre-play preparation phase.
Preprocessor.ruleName: Grammar rule name, used in #useRule directive to define a grammar rule name
Preprocessor.blockName: Block name, used in #block directive to define a block name, and in #use directive to specify which block to call
Preprocessor.versionNumber: Version name, used in #version directive to specify the version of a controller or grammar rule

Background.ImageName: Specifies the image name used for the background
Background.FadeInTime: Specifies the background fade-in time in seconds
Background.StartPosition: Starting coordinate position for background image display
Background.EndPosition: Ending coordinate position for background image display
Background.MoveTime: Time in seconds for the background to move from start to end position
Background.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Background.StitchVertically: Whether to stitch multiple images vertically. true for vertical stitching, false for horizontal stitching.
Background.StitchColumnCount: Maximum stitch count in the current stitching direction; exceeding this count automatically wraps to the next column or row for grid-like stitching

Music.AudioName: Specifies the audio name used for the music
Music.Volume: Specifies the music volume, ranging from 0 to 1
Music.FadeInTime: Specifies the music fade-in time in seconds
Music.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the music, default is 0 (i.e., track index). To play multiple music tracks simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Music.Position: Spatial position of the music, -1 for left, 1 for right, adjustable within this range

SoundEffect.AudioName: Specifies the audio name used for the sound effect
SoundEffect.Volume: Specifies the sound effect volume, ranging from 0 to 1
SoundEffect.Position: Spatial position of the sound effect, -1 for left, 1 for right, adjustable within this range
SoundEffect.Steps: Specifies the number of steps the sound effect lasts, i.e., how many controllers it continues for before terminating

Character.Character&Variant: Specifies the character name and variant; if no variant, use the character name only
Character.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the character, default is 0. To display multiple characters simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Character.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Character.FadeInTime: Specifies the character fade-in time in seconds
Character.FadeOutTime: Specifies the character fade-out time in seconds
Character.RenderOrder: Character rendering order; characters with higher values overlay those with lower values
Character.StartPosition: Starting coordinate position for character display
Character.EndPosition: Ending coordinate position for character display
Character.MoveTime: Time in seconds for the character to move from start to end position

Choice.OutputVariable: Output variable name for the choice; the player's selection result will be stored in this variable

Delay.DelayTime: Duration of the delay in seconds

VisualEffect.VisualEffectName: Specifies the effect name used for the visual effect
VisualEffect.Steps: Specifies the number of steps the visual effect lasts, i.e., how many controllers it continues for before terminating
VisualEffect.Position: Coordinate position of the visual effect
VisualEffect.RenderOrder: Rendering order of the visual effect; higher values overlay lower values

Interlude.Character&Variant: Specifies the character name and variant in the interlude; if no variant, use the character name only
Interlude.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the character in the interlude, default is 0. To display multiple characters simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Interlude.Type: Type of interlude. 0 for gray vertical type, 1 for avatar type
Interlude.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Interlude.RenderOrder: Rendering order of the interlude; higher values overlay lower values

ColorMask.Color: Color of the color mask, using a 4D vector
ColorMask.FadeInTime: Specifies the color mask fade-in time in seconds
ColorMask.StayTime: Specifies the duration the color mask remains in seconds
ColorMask.FadeOutTime: Specifies the color mask fade-out time in seconds
ColorMask.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line

Shake.FadeInTime: Specifies the screen shake fade-in time in seconds
Shake.StayTime: Specifies the duration the screen shake remains in seconds
Shake.FadeOutTime: Specifies the screen shake fade-out time in seconds
Shake.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line

FreeText.IsAddition: Whether it is additional text. Additional text appends to existing text rather than replacing it
FreeText.Immediate: Whether to display text immediately instead of character by character
FreeText.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line
FreeText.FadeInTime: Specifies the text fade-in time in seconds
FreeText.StayTime: Specifies the duration the text remains in seconds
FreeText.FadeOutTime: Specifies the text fade-out time in seconds
FreeText.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the text, default is 0. To display multiple text segments simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
FreeText.AsSeceneObject: Whether to treat the text as a scene object, making it affected by other controllers in the scene such as screen shake and color mask
FreeText.RenderOrder: Rendering order of the text; higher values overlay lower values
FreeText.Interval: Time interval in seconds between each character when displaying character by character
FreeText.ExtraStayTime: Extra duration in seconds after the text is fully displayed when displaying character by character

Cut.Angle: Angle of the cut in degrees
Cut.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the cut, default is 0. To use multiple cuts simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Cut.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Cut.FadeInTime: Specifies the cut fade-in time in seconds
Cut.StayTime: Specifies the duration the cut remains in seconds
Cut.FadeOutTime: Specifies the cut fade-out time in seconds
Cut.RenderOrder: Rendering order of the cut; higher values overlay lower values

Filter.Type&Intensity: Specifies the filter type and intensity; different filter types have different intensity ranges
Filter.FadeInTime: Specifies the filter fade-in time in seconds
Filter.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line

AnimatedText.Type: Type of animated text; different types have different animation effects
AnimatedText.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. A blocking controller waits for all animations to finish before executing the next line

Variable.Name: Variable name
Variable.Expression: Variable expression; the variable's value is evaluated as the result of this expression

Judge.Name: Variable name to store the judgment result
Judge.Expression: Judgment expression; if the calculation result is greater than 0, it is stored in the variable

Dialog.Speaker&Content: Speaker name and dialog text content
Dialog.IsAdditional: Whether it is additional dialog. Additional dialog appends to existing dialog rather than replacing it
Dialog.ModifyCharacters: Whether to synchronously control existing character sprite display to match speaker changes in the dialog, causing character sprites to automatically dim or brighten as the dialog progresses
Dialog.BasicStayTime: Base duration of the dialog in seconds. The dialog will have extra duration based on text length added to this base time
Dialog.Interval: Time interval in seconds between each character when displaying character by character
Dialog.ExtraStayTime: Extra duration in seconds after the text is fully displayed when displaying character by character
Dialog.IsBlocker: Whether it blocks. When this dialog controller is associated with a character sprite, this option controls whether the dialog controller waits for all animations in the character sprite to finish before executing the next line.

Comment.__description: Comment line, not executed by the playback engine.

Aliases.__description: Defines one or more aliases, allowing the dialog controller to automatically highlight corresponding sprites based on the alias.
Aliases.Name: Original name of the sprite material

window.title: ASE-Remake Story Project Initialization Assistant
window.where: The new project will be created in "%1".
window.exists: "%1" already exists or is not empty! Cannot create a new project here.

failed.title: Project Creation Failed
failed.text: Unable to create project. Please check if the target folder is empty, or if you have permission to create new files in this folder.

name.title: Project Name
name.text: The name of the story project. If only path-legal characters are used, it will also be the name of the project folder.

path.title: Project Path
path.text: A new project folder will be created at this path

version.title: Target ASE Version
version.text: Default uses the latest ASE version. If a specific older version is needed, associate the relevant version in the plugin settings.

window.title: AStoryX File Initialization Assistant
window.where: The new file will be created as "%1".
window.exists: "%1" already exists! Cannot create new file.

failed.title: File Creation Failed
failed.text: Unable to create file. Please check if the target folder is empty, or if you have permission to create new files in this folder.

astory.name: ASE Story File
astory.description: ASE story written using AStory, supporting all native ASE features.
astory.title: AStoryX File Initialization

name.title: File Name
name.text: The name of the story file. If only path-legal characters are used, it will also be the name of the file.

path.title: File Path
path.text: A new story file will be created at this path

version.title: Target ASE Version
version.text: Default uses the latest ASE version. If a specific older version is needed, associate the relevant version in the plugin settings.

exePath.title: ASE-Remake Program Path
exePath.text:
Location of the ASE-Remake.exe file.
Properly configure this item to launch ASE-Remake from YSS.

version.title: ASE Version
version.text: The version of the ASE program you selected. If you are unsure of your ASE program's version, or if it is the latest version, select "Latest Version". Correct version settings allow ASER Studio to better integrate with your ASE program and avoid potential issues.

windowSize.title: Window Size
windowSize.text: ASER Studio can specify window size options when launching ASE-Remake; these options are passed directly to the ASE-Remake program.

executableNotFound.title: Unable to Launch ASE-Remake
executableNotFound.message:
The configured ASE-Remake program path "%1" is invalid. Cannot launch ASE-Remake application.
Please check your configuration in File -> Plugins and Preferences -> ASER Studio Settings, ensuring you have correctly set the path to ASE-Remake.exe.

isRunning.title: Seems there is a conflict...
isRunning.message:
Detected that another ASE-Remake program is already running, causing conflicts with the debugger.
The debugger currently does not support connecting to multiple ASE-Remake programs simultaneously. Please close other running ASE-Remake programs before attempting to start debugging.

starting.title: ASE-Remake Beta Defect Notice
starting.message: Due to a known defect in the ASE-Remake beta version, YSS can only launch ASE-Remake simply and cannot correctly set the project directory or playback file. You need to start playback manually. Starting debugging now can only be used as a quick way to open the ASE-Remake program. Please check the ASE-Remake beta version changelog and wait for this issue to be fixed.

notLegalFile.title: Oops, you pressed the wrong button!
notLegalFile.message:
You are attempting to start debugging on file %1, but this is not a valid ASER script file.
Please switch to a .astoryx file before attempting to start debugging.

executing.waitingForPipe: Waiting for communication pipe with ASE-Remake to be established...
executing.waitingForOBMI: Waiting for ASE-Remake to confirm resource initialization status...
executing.waitingForPlay: Waiting for ASE-Remake to start playback...
